mentions.note: Beat Q1 (41% rev growth, 31% margins), held guidance on no Fed cuts; 18th quarter of golden rule of 40. Loan portfolio risk flagged but non-interest revenue ($390M) growing; tech platform thesis intact but rate caution warranted.
Dropped from $30–50 to $15; Cramer didn't like at prior prices, sentiment warmer ('let's run') at current level but still cautious; hold at $15.
First billion-dollar quarter, record revenue, nine straight quarters GAAP profit; CEO Anthony Noto buying shares twice in March; Cramer believes in management and fundamentals.

Digital bank down 30% YTD on AI displacement narrative — wrong thesis. Q4: 37% revenue growth, 160% EPS growth, 35% member growth; 2026 guidance: 30% member growth, 52% net income growth, 54% EPS growth. CEO Noto (30-year track record) built preferred platform for millennials/Gen Z; 40% of new products from existing members. PEG ratio 6.0 (reasonable). Trading 17x 2028 earnings (if guidance beaten as history suggests). Bank won't be disrupted by AI. Buy here.
Down 30% to $18; Cramer flipped positive given growth rate and pullback; was concerned at $26, sees value at current depressed level.
Cramer liked at $5–8, disliked at $28, likes again at $18 after pullback; hanging around $18 level; continue to believe time to buy.
